IBD pedia-
tric in regions where this 
had not been reported in the 
past (8).
In France, according to studies, IBD 
begins in 7 to 25% of cases in 
childhood, with a ratio of 3 cases of 
Crohn’s disease (CD) to 1 case of ulcerative colitis (UC) (9). The distri-
bution of pediatric IBD by sex is the reverse of what it 
is in adults, with a predo-
minance of boys for CD and 
of girls for UC (9). The median age 
at diagnosis of pediatric IBD 
is around 14 years. The incidence 
of pediatric IBD in France has 
increased considerably over 
more than 20 years (10,11).
Risk factors for pediatric IBD
The main risk factor for IBD is the existence of a family 
history of IBD in a first-
degree relative. This association is more common in children than in 
adults, especially the younger the child is at the time 
of IBD diagnosis (12).
The gut microbiota is different in children with 
IBD compared with healthy children. Several of the environmental 
factors associated with the risk of developing 
IBD would act via their impact on the intes-
tinal microbiota.
